title: Jabra Move Style Edition, Black – Wireless Bluetooth Headphones with Superior Sounds Quality, Long Battery Life, Ultra-Light and Comfortable Wireless Headphones, 3.5 mm Jack Connector Included
description: Jabra Move Style Edition is designed for music on the move. These wireless headphones feature the crisp digital sound of Jabra’s signature (Digital Signal Processing) DSP that gives your music, movies and calls true depth and real clarity. The clean, simple, Scandinavian design encompasses a powerhouse of sound and functionality that’s as durable as it is stylish. You can take control of your music and calls directly from these wireless Bluetooth headphones with conveniently placed buttons that allow you to keep your phone in your pocket. Keep the music going all day long. Jabra Move Style Edition has an ultra-light, adjustable headband that stays comfortable, even used over an extended period of time. With up to 14 hours of battery life on a single charge and up to 12 days of standby, these cordless headphones are designed to keep up with your busy day. Plus, a 3.5mm jack connector is included in case you run out of battery or want to listen on an airplane. Jabra Move Style Edition keeps you comfortably taking calls and listening to your music, no matter where you are.
features: Wireless headphones – Jabra move style edition provides unparalleled wireless sound quality. The crisp digital sound of Jabra’s signature (Digital Signal Processing) DSP gives your music, movies and calls true depth and real clarity wherever you are.

Multi-functional Bluetooth headphones – Take control of your music and calls directly from the headphones. Conveniently placed buttons mean you can control your calls and music at the touch of a button and enjoy the true freedom of wireless technology.
All day comfort – An ultra-light, adjustable headband means you can wear the move style edition all day long. So you can comfortably chat and listen to music for hours.

Durable design – The clean, simple, Scandinavian design of move style edition encompasses a powerhouse of sound and functionality that’s as durable as it is stylish. These cordless headphones are designed to inspire and built to last.

Up to 14 hours of use – The Move Style Edition is designed to keep up with your busy day. Enjoy up to 14 hours of battery life on a single charge and up to 12 days of standby. If you run out of battery, simply attach the 3.5 millimeter cable (included standard).

reviews.title: Good value for the price
reviews.text:
First I like sound! I'm an audiophile at heart but in my life now I'm an audiophile on a BUDGET! These are my first bluetooth speakers. More on that in a minute.

I was looking for on ear bluetooth speakers with a budget of about $50 to $60 and was set on waiting for a sale on the JBL EB45T but then saw these recommended on the wirecutter website (a pretty good review site in my opinion)

There is nothing wrong with these, but nothing really right about them either. They have a nice neutral sound that you can listen to for a long time. For movies and youtube on my computer, they are great. But there is nothing in the sound that is going to wow you either. Trust me if there was something actually wrong with the sound, I'd be telling you. You can listen to these for a long time without ear fatigue. If you are looking for lots of rumble for movies, these probably aren't the right phones for you. But if your movie listening requirements are more modest then these work well for that. For casual background music, podcasts, audiobooks, they will work really well for that.

Isolation is not great. I think the plastic is thin so a certain amount of noise gets in. I wanted to be able to hear a little bit of background noise, things going on in the house, but these push my limit for how much I want to hear. Even though they were open-back my Sennheiser HD-229 did a better job with isolation because of the design of the pad.

Comfort isn't bad and for my ears, the ear cup size is just about right size. I wish the earpieces swiveled a bit more but for my head, they swivel just enough. . They have a left and right and the cups swivel so little that if you have them on the wrong way you will notice.

Bluetooth... These are a pain in the backside on Win 7. When I want to use them I have to go and delete the previous pairing, pair them again and then go to the control panel and set these to be used for output. For movies on the computer, they get out of sync. I even set my movie player (Pot Player which I highly recommend) to use the Bluetooth headphones and they still get out of sync with the audio. I don't know if it's the player, the USB Bluetooth adapter, Windows 7 or some combination of them together.

I like that they can be used with a wire as well as with bluetooth. The controls are a little small, as mentioned by others but I don't have trouble working them.

I also tried the JBL EB45T Bluetooth headphones, which cost almost twice as much, at Target and these sound better overall. The JBL might have had better highs, a little punchier bass but these are more cohesive across the frequency range with nothing tweaked to make them sound 'better'.

Summary: I don't have any other Bluetooth headphones to compare these to but I think most people would be very satisfied with these at this price point for general use. If however, you are looking for audiophile on a budget, it's not here.

reviews.title: The sound quality is fantastic and the battery life is excellent
reviews.text: I've been very impressed by many things in the month that I've had this.  The sound quality is fantastic and the battery life is excellent.  I've owned these for a month and the leather cover over the ear cushion is splitting exposing the foam.  Not sure if this is a fluke or what kind of flaw this is.  I've just been using them at my desk and walking around town, medium use for about 5 weeks, when I noticed it was splitting.

reviews.title: Good sound effects
reviews.text: Good sound effects, but Bluetooth connection is not very reliable, the sound is intermittent, you have to turn it off and repair it with other devices like laptop, iPhone, egg.

reviews.title: These headphones are incredible.
reviews.text: Fantastic value. Amazing sound, light and comfortable. I never write reviews but I was so pleased with this product that I did.

reviews.title: Meh... just OK.  Not worth $100
reviews.text: Read some good reviews and picked it up on sale during prime sale.  To me, these are just OK.  I'm not overly impressed.  For $100 they are not worth it, less than $60, just OK.  I have a pair of motorola solo wireless and those are much better as it pertains to battery life, sound, and durability.  These seem like they are going to break very easily.  They feel extremely cheap.  The sound is not bad but nothing overly impressive.  Battery life is meh, it's giving low battery warnings at 50% levels based on what is reported to my iPhone, Plays that warning message about every 5 minutes or so... stupid.  When I turn it on it takes a bit for it to function with my iPhone 7, Motorola solo pretty much instantly ready.  On a positive note, I've used them with phone calls and no one complains... my Motorola solo there are a few times where people had issues hearing me.

title: USB C Hub, Baseus 6-in-1 Portable USB C Adapter for iPad Pro, Upgrade Type C Dongle to 4K HDMI, USB 3.0, SD/Micro SD Card Reader, 3.5mm Earphone Jack, Type C PD Charger, Independent Switch
description:
It easily extends the USB-C port of your laptop to 4K HDMI, 2 USB 3.0 Port, TF Card Reader, fast USB-C PD charging port and 3.5mm Headphone Jack. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary.
Features:
Turns a single USB-C connection into 6 ports (3.5mm Audio Jack, 2 USB 3.0, Micro SD, USB-C, HDMI) Take full advantage of iPadOS with the hub that supports both USB-C and USB-A flash/hard storage drives. Connect to USB-A peripherals via high speed USB 3.1 Gen 1 capable of 5Gb/s transfer speed. Use hub and charge iPad Pro at the same time! Also supports USB-C data. Simply connect with the HDMI port, mirrors your laptop's display (supports 4K at 30Hz ) on any HDMI-enabled device. Dual USB 3.0 ports, transmission rate up to 5Gbps, can transfer a high-definition movie in seconds. 3.5mm Audio and Mic 2 in 1 Port makes listening, talking, or recording a breeze. Built in TF slot for easy access to files from universal Micro SD Memory Card(SDXC: with high storage capacity up to 2TB)
Specification:
Material: Aluminum alloy Colour: Deep gray Input:Dual Type-C  Output: USB3.0*2+HDMI*1+PD*1+3.5mm*1+TF*1 USB3.0 transmission speed: 5Gbps HDMI Resolution: 4K@30Hz  PD input power: 100W Max.  TF Card: Supports data retrieval from 2TB Memory Card  3.5mm Audio Frequency Port: Port supports standard US headphone sets Size: 83*36*19mm Weight: 65g
Package List:
1* USB C Hub Adapter 1* User Manual
features: Creative Mobile Hub - The Newest Version 6-in-1 USB-C Hub instantly turns your iPad Pro and laptop USB-C port to a 4K HDMI port, 2 USB 3.0 Ports, a USB C charging port with power delivery, 3.5mm Audio/mic 2-in-1 jack (CTIA) and a TF card reader. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary. (NOTE: Only iPad Pro of iOS 13 or above is supported.)

Fast Charge & Data Transfer - The USB-C PD charging port supports pass-through charging of up to 60W, quickly charges iPad Pro while using the other ports, and can connect USB C flash drive for data transfer. USB 3.0 supports transfer speeds up to 5Gbps, fast enough to transfer and share images, files, and videos in seconds.

Crystal Clear 4K Resolution - Mirror or extend your screen with the USB C adapter’s HDMI port and directly stream 4K 3840*2160 @ 30Hz or Full HD 1080p video to HDTV, monitors, or projectors for a cinema-like visual feast. (Tips: Video output for 2018 iPad Pro is 2K@60HZ resolution). And the 3.5mm headphones Jack ensures you talk and listen to music at the same time, keep your focus on call or music.

Pad/Laptop Matching Design - Align the L-shaped holder against the corner of your pad or other USB-C devices, Baseus USB C hub can be securely attached. It is recommended not to wear a protective cover or other accessories that affect the thickness of the device. (such as magic keyboard)

Universal Compatibility Compatible with new iPad Pro 2020/2019/2018, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air 2019/2018, MacBook, Google Chromebook, Dell XPS 13 15, Lenovo Yoga 900, HP Spectre notebook, Surface Book 2, Google Pixel 3 XL/3, Samsung Galaxy Note 10/Note 9/S9/S8/Note 8/, Huawei Mate 30/20/M10/P30/P20, and more USB-C laptop/smartphone.

reviews.title: Don’t buy this knock off, get the real deal
reviews.text: This is the knock-off brand. The real version of this has an SD & micro SD card slot. This version also stopped working for me in under a month. I first noticed it wouldn’t regularly connect to my monitor via the HDMI port, then it began failing to connect all together. The power pass through on this is awful, charging takes forever, but it does with apples dongles also so whatever. Get the real deal & save 50 bucks

reviews.title: I wanted to love this,
reviews.text: I got this for my new iPad. It’s supposed to let you use wired headphones, SIM cards, and even hdmi.  And it does.  It’s a bit cumbersome to use but it does sit on the corner of my iPad even with the iPad cover on.  The big issue i have with it is that it does get hot, VERY HOT, while you’re using it for a longer period of time.  I don’t use it often due to that but i got it at a good price so I’ll keep it.

reviews.title: So far it works as advertised!  Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity..
reviews.text:
update::: I've been having issues with connecting this hub to a capture device. it will constantly disconnect and reconnect. I purchased an hdmi splitter that has it's own power source (micro usb) and I connect it with the ipad and the capture device. It's like a middle man... and now the this hub stops disconnecting and reconnecting. It's very odd. Also, connecting power via the USB C port, on the hub, does not help. It's also not a heat issue because I bought a mini fan to blow on it and it's never hot. However, it doesn't disconnect and reconnect when directly connected to a monitor/tv.. it works fine then.

in addition to that issue, I had to get a replacement and noticed that both hubs displayed different color profiles. Using the same monitor, cables, ipad, etc. One was sort of washed out and one was very saturated. Also one wouldn't display 4k (only 2k) and the other one would display 4k, 2k, 1080p, etc. Not really sure why there would be so much variance with the same product.. I guess QC issues?

-----------------------------------

This is the "UPGRADED" version.

So far it works as advertised! Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity of the hub itself and concerned about potential damage this could do to my iPad...

This product advertises "Heat vents" but they're just cutouts for the ipad's speakers.. it doesn't actually vent the inside of the hub. This seems deceptive.

I've tried those $20-30 cheap usb c to hdmi + charging port options and they all didn't work properly... mostly they wouldn't actually charge the ipad while outputting the HDMI signal.
This device actually DOES charge while outputting the HDMI signal properly.. which is great.
The audio port works fine, but I have not used the USB ports as I don't really have any use for them at the moment. I mostly needed hdmi output and a charging port that actually charged while hdmi was being used.

I love how the company included 3 different sized spacers to accommodate for ipad's with cases.

The switch to disable the hub is a nice feature. BUT I wish you could disable the charging while still being able to use the HDMI out... so if this switch instead only disabled the charging capabilities but still retained the ability to output video/audio via the HDMI output, that would be, imo, better. Then when the ipad's own battery gets low.. I could flip the switch and allow the hub to start charging. Obviously I can just unplug the USB C charging cable from the hub, but it would be a QoL feature to not have to do that.

I like how it has a long-ish cable instead of just having the usb port extend out of the actual hub body.. this makes it much more versatile, imo.

My biggest concern is how long this product will last due to how HOT it gets while using the HDMI out and charging the ipad at the same time. This is the only time the hub gets alarmingly HOT.. when one is using the HDMI out AND charging at the same time. One could argue that because it's so hot to the touch, that means the passive cooling, inside the hub, is doing its job.. but I don't know if that's actually the case w/o seeing the internals and seeing what kind of heatsinks or w/e are being used. All I know is that this hub gets VERY VERY HOT and devices that overheat tend to stop working sooner than later.

Overall I think this is one of the better options available for 30-50$. The versatility of attaching to the ipad or have it hanging is a huge plus for me.

reviews.title: Convenient & Useful iPad adapter
reviews.text:
So far the only bummer is it’s not really screen protector friendly. I used the thinnest insert because without one it won’t stay in place.

It’s a bit too snug to put on an iPad 11 with a screen protector. The edge of the protector kinda bubbles up. I’ll have to try a few thin cases and not use the inserts to this adapter. That would be more beneficial anyways.

One thing I did notice about the charging port is it’s a bit loose. So you may need to get new cables or try different brands. I have several brands/styles and some do better than others. The Apple cables seem to fit fairly snug though.

Other than that it works as stated. I do like it and it’s more low profile compared to others. All of the ports work especially for charging multiple devices. I use 30w+ for my iPad because I tend to use adapters often. 30w brick & adapter loaded still charged iPad under use.

The adapter looks nice as well. A bit thick but not to the point it’s an issue using it. I have the model with the on/off switch. That’s convenient while otg, in case you need to cut power from the adapter to save the battery from draining.

The adapter doesn’t seem to take a ton of power but if you don’t need it on while away from a power source I’d say just flip it off.

Overall I like this adapter it’s nice & convenient. Like I said my biggest gripe was not really screen protector friendly (iPad w/out a case only screen protector) but it’s not a deal breaker. I’d recommend trying this out!

reviews.text:
MONOCARBON came in hot with this lite weight protective carbon fiber shell. When opening the box I was kind of surprised the presentation looked really nice. In the box was included ( Carbon Fiber Shell, Lanyard, Cards with info on it, even included a little piece of carbon fiber to keep)

The shell weight is about 8 grams, and is 0.6mm thick. The hole cutouts are precise. Seems very durable and looks like it would withstand some drops and help protect from scratches when it's inside your pocket. The include lanyard is a nice touch and it makes use with the built in lanyard spot in the AirPods itself.

I overall would recommend this product for your EDC especially if you like carbon fiber and light weight / non bulky cases.

reviews.title: A very minimal sleek case for the airPods Pro 2
reviews.text:
I have purchased this brand before for my apple watch series 6 titanium, Ive only had the shell for two days before it decided to walk away, or fell off and went byby. There was no adhesion to the watch and little effort will cause it to come off. Thank you guys for your concern about what we think about your product. Great customer service!

With that said, This case is no different with the top half, it will slip off with out you knowing.

They have no adhesive strips like others will include. I guess they have to high of confidence in their product. Nice as it is, I would expect more.

I have purchase thin strip double sided adhesive through amazon to have around when needed. This was no exception.

Now that I had to vent my concern with their product, I was prepared with handling this concern.

The bottom shell is no problem since the opening for the lanyard will secure the bottom shell, I have added a small strip of double sided adhesive to the sides of the top half shell.

These are the most minimal light weight hardshell cases you can buy, the look great and feel’s great and will take any reasonable
punishment you can give it and
protect what is important.

The packaging is nice, too nice, they could have spent less on the box and a little more on protecting the investment, like adhesive. They do have decals in a strip, if this was to prevent cases from slipping off, you could have fooled me. There was no instructions in the box on anything, 🤔.

Don’t let my concern detour you from making the investment.

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

reviews.title: Big Lag
reviews.text: I use this headset for working from home, and every phone call is a struggle due to lag. I am constantly talking over people or being talked over by them. It's functional, but not ideal perhaps for phone calls.

reviews.title: Big Upgrade for me!!
reviews.text: These are fantastic!  I had a pair that pooped out in less than a year, I got these with my fingers crossed and so far they are a BIG UPGRADE!!! Voice clarity & noise canceling are great!  I work from home with 2 big dogs, it matters for me.

reviews.title: Very happy
reviews.text: I really like this headset.  I love the charging station as I often woukd forget to charge my last headset that was charged with usb cable.  My only issue with the headset is that the headband is not adjustable and I have a smaller head so the support bar sits a little low.  Very good connection and stays connected if I move around the house.

reviews.title: It Won't Charge
reviews.text: It worked wonderfully for a couple months and now it won't charge. I have switched outlets thinking it was my outlet. I also make sureits lined up in charging base. I loved it until it stopped working. Its a little pricey to only get 2 month use.

reviews.title: Comfortable for truckers and the office.
reviews.text:
I am a dispatcher and I have to wear a headset 8hrs a day. I can stand this one for about 6 hrs. before I need to switch ears. That is better than most headsets I have used. My office orders $300 headsets and this one can throw down with the best of them. The noise cancelation is excellent and the calls sound loud and clear. The Bluetooth connection last the entire 8+ hour shift. Once paired it connects seamlessly when I set up for my day.

Before you spend hundreds of dollars on a headset, give this one a chance. I am glad I did.

I hope this helps. :-)

reviews.title: [review for non ANC version] Pretty good deal for it's money
reviews.text:
I lost accurate track of time, but it was definitely more than 60hr of battery life. very impressive, a little scary to carry this much power on my head, but logically I understand that risks of catching fire or batteries exploding is negligible)

* sound quality - awesome (in terms of sound itself)
* build and material quality - impressive
* bluetooth connectivity - awesome. range, stability, reconnect speed, sound quality (in terms of losses via bluetooth while in varying range of "hotspot with music").
* battery quality - a little bit over a week of daily uses for 8hr+ a day. just perfect. That will be enough for a business trip out of town for a couple of days and back without need to recharge and sometimes even without prior notice to recharge before I go.
* distinct controls (bumps with space between them) - awesome. easy to use, easy to locate, no mistakes)
* folding capabilities - they do fold/unfold, but I tried it only once upon unpacking, but I rarely use this feature

The only minus that I can see in the last 20 days is how the headband is stretching when I'm wearing headphones. Looks dangerous (in terms of longevity). That's all I think. If this headphones will last more than a year I would call them perfect for a price and capabilities (tiny note about non ANC version and that's it)

So final score (while they last) 10/10

reviews.title: It is not passive soundproofing it is active. Which is NOT what I need.
reviews.text:
I have difficulty finding headphones with passive noise canceling for whatever reason and so I saw that this listing said passive and it was from the same brand as one I am still using. (It's breaking down due to age)

Active soundproofing is extremely detrimental to someone with audio disorders like myself since it makes a low buzzing noise that I can't tune out.

It does work as headphones but I would like to have it refunded so I can get another pair with the format of soundproofing I need.

reviews.title: AWESOME HEADPHONES!!
reviews.text: AWESOME headphones for music or phone calls!! Pairs up with my phone, usually within seconds. I listen to tons of YouTube music videos with these and the sound rivals more expensive sets. For the price on these you just can't go wrong!! Battery life is amazing too!!!

reviews.title: Excellent, but …..
reviews.text: Good quality sound and bluetooth……only con is they don’t keep much outside noise out including wind.

reviews.title: Decent, however.
reviews.text: I will be contacting the seller in regards to these concerns, though the Bluetooth connection to my phone is poor and seems to only want to have stable connection when i have my phone in my hands or bra. I don't feel these go as loudly as they should, when being used for exercise.

title: Audio Transmitter Receiver TV Computer Speaker Car Adapter 3.5mm Bluetooth 5.0 2-in-1 for Home Sound System/TV/PC/Tablet Headphone General (Black) (Large)
description:
Switch mode:
(1) RX: Receive mode, indicator: blue LED  (2) TX: Transmit mode, indicator light: red LED
RX mode:
Pairing name: KN321, find KN321 in the Bluetooth settings of the phone to pair. The blue light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the blue light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
how to use:
(1) Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio input of the speaker and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)confirm that it is in RX mode (blue light flash). Find the pairing name in the Bluetooth settings of the phone: KN321 click to pair.
TX mode:
TX mode will automatically search for other Bluetooth receiving devices (such as Bluetooth headset, Bluetooth speaker, Bluetooth receiver, etc.), The red light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the red light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
How to use:
(1)Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio output of the devices and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)Confirm that it is in TX mode (red light flash). The KN321 will automatically search for Bluetooth headset or Bluetooth speaker pairing.
features: ★[BLUETOOTH TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER 2IN1] Receiver mode: wirelessly streams audio from your Bluetooth-enabled phone, tablet, or computer to your wired stereo, speakers, or headphones (Only support to be paired with one Bluetooth receiver). Transmitter mode: wirelessly streams audio from your non-Bluetooth TV, home stereo system, or CD player to your Bluetooth headphones or speakers. Switch between transmitter and receiver modes with the simple flick of a switch.
★[NO DRIVER REQUIRED PLUG READY TO USE] No need to download driver, plug and play TV computer or Bluetooth speaker headset.

★[PORTABLE AUDIO-STREAMING POWER] Bluetooth Adapter has got powerful wireless audio streaming capabilities. It features Bluetooth 5.0 chip to ensure high fidelity transmission of Bluetooth audio signal for music streaming to those speakers or home stereo systems that doesn’t have the capability.Slip it into your backpack or laptop bag and take it on-the-go without the slightest effort.

★[WIDE APPLICATION] With the included 3.5mm cable and 3.5mm to 2RCA cable, this Receiver Transmitter adapter can widely be used on your computer, laptop, home stereo system, headphone, smartphone, Mp3 player, CD player, Cars with AUX-in and 3.5mm connector/ Computer Speakers/ Odinary Speakers/ Subwoofers/ Radios/ Multimedia Speakers/ Power Amplifiers/ Echo Walls, etc.

★[QUALITY ASSURANCE AND SERVICE] Provides a 60-day return and 12-month replacement warranty, as well as lifetime accessible technical support. Please contact us if you have any questions. Reply to your message within 12 hours.

reviews.title: Great quality device if you're on a budget
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:e49e95641bb1dc426258e889516d11e0]] Over great product for the price. I can't complain. If you've got the money and would like to go for a more "high end" product there are plenty of options available. However, this one appeals greater to the general public. Most reviews say the same thing: It cuts out if too far away and it has a short battery life. Also if it is being used while charging you'll heard a little buzzing noise. You get what you pay for. It does its job and I can't complain  for the price.

reviews.title: For A/V wireless solution, it is workable but not perfect.
reviews.text:
First, my purpose is to stream audio to wired QC headphones from my AV receiver, as a replacement for my broken analog FM TV sound transmitter/phones. For THIS purpose... I bought a pair of these BT devices, and it works, but the sync between audio and video is - versus analog - perceptible. The streaming generation is less sensitive to this, but AV-philes find it annoying.

It is a problem in all BT, compressed audio, technologies - to some degree. Compressed audio requires buffering, buffering introduces some minimum delay, and the delay can be worse depending upon the nature of the audio.
For many programs (sports, etc) sync isn't a problem, but in close-up talky-talky scenes the audio lag is annoying vs. the FM phones, which have imperceptible lag. For NON-SYNCed applications - this is a non-issue.
I wish Roku had a AV sync setting (+/- ms). This is what BT 5.0 does - it delays the video based on the estimated audio lag. *But that doesn't help here because these devices do NOT control the video stream*.

Otherwise, audio quality of BT vs FM is equivalent. My FM phones were very good (AR, $150) and audio was excellent, so the BT pair has good sound in this application. I have not noted any static others reported, except for a slight hum when the RX is not connected. Generally, the BT audio is equivalent to any other BT audio connection (ie., streaming iPhone to car stereo, etc.).
8/18/2020 Revision: After a power outage, I noticed a VERY bad hum. Removed RX side's power, no change. Removed TX sides power (from USB outlet on cable box), NO HUM. I tried several other USB outlets, each had some hum though with different qualities. Finally, I got out a good high amp USB hub & used it to power the TX side - no hum at all. SO... the transmitting side is VERY sensitive to ripple in its USB power circuitry.

One VERY annoying thing - my devices both power off after 4hrs (6hrs?) regardless of input, even though both TX/RX devices are permanently wired to USB power. It ought to power down only if on battery or unconnected for some time (firmware update perhaps?). Other than this obvious design choice, I have had no connection issues - power on TX, then RX, connects reliably in ~10s. Once connected stays reliably connected UNTIL it powers down.

So the good: Cheap solution to use superior wired headphones. Small & USB powered, so hides away easily on both TX and RX sides. Cheaper & more versatile than quality FM TV transmitter/phone combos.

And the bad: Audio LAGS behind video noticeably, 50-150ms. Also, I haven't tried with multiple BT sources - but there is no UI, no way to select a specific source - I suspect this would be a problem for some.

Overall: For the price, I can't complain and wouldn't spend 5x for good FM TV audio phones.

reviews.title: Compatible With All Devices
reviews.text: Amazing product! I like the easy connectivity and sound quality is top of the line. I wish had known about this product before. It is compatible with all of my devices. Even my older television! Five stars all the way!

reviews.title: Nope. Didn't work with TV..
reviews.text: I bought this to try to move the sound FROM my Panasonic, non-bluetooth television TO my bluetooth headphones or a bluetooth speaker. Tried both, neither worked - was a complete waste of time. Now, who's going to refund me for the printer ink to print a label to return it?

reviews.title: Works like a charm
reviews.text: Fantastic. Works right out of the box. I set it up with an aux to an older stereo and it connected right away. I'm probably going to buy another one for the office.

reviews.title: Fit perfect
reviews.text: Face some reason I was expecting these to be a different material than they were but I’m actually glad with the material that they are and how they fit perfectly on my AirPods.

reviews.title: Yellow
reviews.text: They turned yellow almost immediately

reviews.title: Yellowing?
reviews.text: I’ve had these for less than a month and really love them. Easy to put on and stay put. Today I noticed that one of covers is starting to yellow. I don’t typically keep my headphones anywhere except on my head or in the case, so i have not gotten them dirty to cause this. Anyone else?

reviews.title: Must Buy
reviews.text: Excellent product. Highly recommend to protect a very expensive headphones. Quick delivery.

reviews.title: Would freely recommend
reviews.text: Item works as described. It’s very slim, barely adds to the bulk of the AirPods Max. I would recommend this product to anyone looking for a bespoke way to protect their investment.
